title: Jalapeño Popper Smashburger
imagePrompt: Two ultra-thin smashed patties are stacked on a toasted pretzel bun, their deeply browned and craggy crusts embedded with jalapeño slices softened and charred at the edges. Pale cream cheese spreads beneath the patties while a thick smear of deep-red raspberry bacon jam coats the top bun — its vivid color bleeding into the ivory cream cheese where the bun compresses, with melted Havarti cascading down the sides. Shot on a near-black weathered slate or matte dark surface, with a low, dramatic ¾ front angle that shows the full height of the burger stack and the cheese pull. Single hard side-light from the left casts deep shadow that makes the molten Havarti drips and jam smear pop. Bold, pub-editorial.
subtitle: Smashed beef patties with jalapeños, Havarti, whipped cream cheese, and raspberry bacon jam on a pretzel bun.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Jam takes 45–50 min to reduce — start it well before the burgers.
items: 1 lb bacon, diced
1 white onion, diced
1 double shot espresso (or ¼ cup black coffee)
2–3 tbsp apple cider vinegar
¼ cup brown sugar
1 cup fresh raspberries
Salt & pepper to taste
1–1½ lbs ground beef, formed into 4 oz balls
2–3 jalapeños, thinly sliced
Texas Jalapeño Salt (or jalapeño seasoning + kosher salt)
4–8 slices Havarti cheese
2–4 pretzel buns
4 oz whipped cream cheese
ingredients.callout: Make the jam first: It needs 45–50 minutes to reduce. Get it going, then prep everything else.

body.html: A jalapeño popper-inspired smashburger: two crispy-edged beef patties with fresh jalapeños smashed right in, melted Havarti, whipped cream cheese, and a slow-cooked raspberry bacon jam that's sweet, smoky, and tangy.

items: Cook the bacon for the jam
Build the raspberry bacon jam — simmer 45–50 min
Smash patties with jalapeños pressed in before flipping
Melt the Havarti and pull the burgers
Assemble with cream cheese on the bottom, jam on top

body.html: Timing tip: Start the jam first — it's mostly hands-off but needs time. The burgers take under 5 minutes total.

slides.title: Cook the Bacon
slides.checkboxLabel: Bacon crispy with slight chew, drippings reserved

items: Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high.
Add diced bacon; cook 10–15 minutes until crispy with a slight chew.
Drain and remove the bacon — leave the drippings in the pan.
Set cooked bacon aside to return in the next step.

body.html: Keep the drippings. The onions and jam build directly in that fat — don't wipe the pan.

slides.title: Build the Raspberry Bacon Jam
slides.checkboxLabel: Jam reduced and thickened, set aside

items: In the same pan with drippings, sauté diced onion for 3 minutes.
Add espresso, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, and raspberries.
Return cooked bacon to the pan; season with salt & pepper.
Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer 45–50 minutes until jam thickens.
Set aside — holds well at room temp while you cook the burgers.

body.html: Done when: Jam coats a spoon and looks sticky and spreadable. It tightens more as it cools.

slides.title: Smash the Patties
slides.checkboxLabel: Patties smashed with jalapeños, dark crust formed

items: Heat griddle or cast-iron over medium-high until very hot.
Place a 4 oz beef ball on the griddle.
Lay jalapeño slices on top before smashing — they press into the meat.
Smash flat immediately with a burger press or spatula — hard and firm, one motion.
Season the top with Jalapeño Salt.
Cook ~90 seconds without moving — you want a dark crust.

body.html: Smash with confidence: One firm press all the way down. Hesitating or re-pressing kills the crust.

items: Flip patties after ~90 seconds.
Immediately top with Havarti.
Cook 30 more seconds as cheese melts.
Pull and stack two patties per burger.

body.html: Havarti melts fast. 30 seconds is enough — pull before it runs off the edge.

slides.title: Assemble
slides.checkboxLabel: Cream cheese bottom, patties stacked, jam on top bun

items: Spread whipped cream cheese generously on the bottom bun.
Stack two patties on the cream cheese.
Spoon raspberry bacon jam on the top bun.
Press together and serve immediately.

body.html: Order matters: Cream cheese on the bottom anchors the patties; jam on the top bun coats every bite evenly.
